description: Redesign owner, room, session, slash, thinking, model, login, fork/clone, and unsupported-action pickers as compact terminal overlays with Web-aligned action semantics and robust keyboard behavior.

userStories.title: Implement shared compact overlay style
userStories.description: As a TUI user, I want all pickers to feel like one terminal command surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Owner, room, session, slash, thinking, model, login, fork/clone, and confirmation/unavailable overlays share a compact terminal style
Overlay rows use short title, selected marker such as ❯, primary label first, dim secondary metadata, disabled/unavailable marker, and compact control hint
Long owner/room/session IDs are secondary, dimmed, abbreviated, or moved to details; they are not dominant primary text
Startup overlays may appear before transcript rows but still use the same terminal overlay grammar
Overlay rendering does not permanently append picker UI to transcript history unless an action/result is produced
Focused overlay renderer tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Implemented one compact Ink overlay grammar for slash suggestions and picker states: lowercase terse title, ❯ selected marker, primary label first, dim/secondary metadata via · separators, × disabled marker, and compact ↑↓/enter/esc/ctrl-c hints. Verified with npm run build; focused node --test test/cli-ui-session-app.test.mjs test/cli-ui-ink-renderer.test.mjs test/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.test.mjs test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck; npm test. PTY evidence: /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17/owner-room-session-message, slash-suggestions-status-thinking, overlay-keyboard-model-login. Evidence tier: deterministic debug/local PTY plus unit/source tests; each PTY dir has raw.ansi.log, clean.txt, screen.txt, metadata.json, input.json, assertions.json, events.jsonl. Visual conversion remains the documented fallback from the existing audit/PRD07 work.

userStories.title: Redesign owner, room, and session navigation pickers
userStories.description: As an SSH operator, I want to navigate users, rooms, and sessions by names and state instead of scanning UUIDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Owner picker shows Web users and Root recovery with display name primary and owner scope secondary/dim/abbreviated
Switching owner clears invalid active room/session and this behavior is covered by controller tests
Room picker shows title primary, default-room marker, session count or empty state, and non-dominant room id metadata
Session picker shows title primary plus compact status/profile/model/updated metadata where available and includes + New session for empty rooms
Back/Escape from session picker returns to room picker without corrupting selected indices or input
Focused descriptor/controller/render tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Owner/room/session picker rows now keep display names/titles primary and move owner scopes, room ids, session ids, status/profile/model/updated metadata to abbreviated secondary text; + New session is compact; /room uses select-room behavior; room-to-session navigation stores the room picker as parent so Escape backs from session picker to room picker. Controller/render coverage in test/cli-ui-session-app.test.mjs covers owner switching guardrails, room-first /session and /room pickers, Escape parent behavior, create-session flows, and compact render output. PTY evidence: owner-room-session-message and overlay-keyboard-model-login under /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17. Evidence tier: deterministic debug/local PTY plus focused unit/render tests.

userStories.title: Implement thinking, model, and login keyboard overlays
userStories.description: As a Web user moving to SSH, I want model, thinking, and login actions to preserve familiar command semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Thinking picker uses shared thinking levels, shows current/default state, and direct /thinking high still works
Model picker preserves provider-to-model flow, shows provider/model availability, disabled reasons, and executes the same action semantics as Web descriptors
Login picker preserves provider-to-auth-method flow and renders device/browser URL or API-key instructions as terminal-safe rows/instructions
API-key or secret input paths never echo secrets into transcript, artifacts, logs, or state.message
Nested overlays support Up/Down, Enter, Escape/back one level, and Ctrl+C clean exit
Focused descriptor/controller/render tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Thinking, model, and login overlays use the same compact command-menu renderer; direct /thinking high and direct /model or /login arguments still append command-result rows; nested provider-to-model and provider-to-auth-method menus support Enter and Escape/back; API-key output stays terminal-safe and does not echo secrets. Verified by Slash /thinking, /model, and /login controller tests plus overlay-keyboard-model-login PTY. Redaction grep over PRD06 clean/screen artifacts found no unredacted secret-shaped values. Evidence tier: deterministic debug/local PTY plus unit/controller tests.

userStories.title: Handle fork, clone, browser-only, and unsupported actions compactly
userStories.description: As a user, I want unavailable and adapted Web actions to have clear terminal-native outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Fork/clone candidates use keyboard selection with title/session metadata primary and ids secondary
/clone opens the derived session when available while preserving a visible command/result row in the originating flow
Browser-only upload/download or unsupported actions show concise unavailable reasons in the same overlay/row style
Disabled/unavailable items are visible and cannot be executed silently without feedback
Unsupported action output is not mistaken for transcript history unless it is an explicit command result row
Focused unsupported/fork/clone tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Fork candidates, clone/session-link outcomes, browser-only/download unavailable results, unsupported slash suggestions, and disabled model provider items render with compact command rows or overlay rows rather than silent/no-feedback states. Existing command-result transcript flow is preserved for /clone and unsupported actions. Verified by Slash /fork-candidates and Slash Web action commands tests, compact overlay disabled-row render test, slash-suggestions-status-thinking PTY for /download unavailable result, and overlay-keyboard-model-login PTY for disabled provider feedback. Evidence tier: deterministic debug/local PTY plus focused controller/render tests.

userStories.title: Validate overlay keyboard flows through PTY
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want terminal artifacts proving nested navigation and command execution work visually and behaviorally.
acceptanceCriteria: PTY startup flow captures owner/room/session picker screens that are compact and human-readable

PTY scenarios cover slash palette, /owner or owner switch where feasible, /room, /session, /thinking, and at least one /model or /login nested flow with deterministic provider data where live data is unavailable
PTY scenarios test Escape/back behavior, Enter confirmation, disabled/unavailable item rendering, and Ctrl+C clean exit
PTY scenario runs /status while a picker is open and proves the picker closes/suspends and transcript command/status rows append correctly
Artifacts include raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, metadata, input/assertions, exact commands, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Focused PTY overlay tests pass
Full npm test passes before this PRD is marked complete
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. PTY scenarios captured startup owner/room/session picker flow, slash palette, /thinking, nested /model and /login flows, Escape/back, Enter confirmation, disabled/unavailable row rendering, Ctrl+C clean exit, and /status while a picker is open. Artifact dirs: /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17/owner-room-session-message; /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17/slash-suggestions-status-thinking; /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17/overlay-keyboard-model-login. Each has raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, events, metadata, input, and assertions. Commands: npm run build; focused node --test test/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.test.mjs test/cli-ui-session-app.test.mjs test/cli-ui-ink-renderer.test.mjs test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; node scripts/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.mjs --scenario owner-room-session-message --artifact-root .tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd06-2026-05-17; same for slash-suggestions-status-thinking and overlay-keyboard-model-login; npm run typecheck; npm test (675 tests). Classification: deterministic debug/local PTY, not live-provider; final installed/global smoke remains PRD07.
